articles.title: S&P Global resets Nike’s stock forecast
articles.summary: S&P Global has revised Nike's stock outlook to negative, while HSBC downgraded it to hold, slashing its price target. This follows Nike's weak Q4 guidance and falling profits, leading investors to question the pace of its turnaround. The company is facing challenges from declining sales in its direct channels, weakness in China, and tariff pressures, despite a positive rebound in its wholesale segment.
articles.text_to_analyze: S&P Global resets Nike’s stock forecast. S&P Global has revised Nike's stock outlook to negative, while HSBC downgraded it to hold, slashing its price target. articles.title: NIKE, Inc. Class B Trade Ideas — XETR:NKE
articles.summary: This article compiles various trading ideas and technical analyses for NIKE, Inc. (NKE) on TradingView. Contributors present diverse perspectives ranging from bullish long-term outlooks based on brand strength, potential for seasonal reversals, and accumulation patterns, to bearish short-term views due to technical breakdowns and fundamental weaknesses like declining revenue and free cash flow. Several analyses highlight key price levels, support/resistance zones, and potential targets, offering both buy and hold recommendations with varying risk assessments for traders.

articles.title: Cramer Highlights Nike, Amazon, Nvidia Stock To Investors: 'Why Would You Sell It?'
articles.summary: Jim Cramer discussed three top stocks from the CNBC Investing Club portfolio: Nike, Amazon, and Nvidia. Despite having buyer's remorse for Nike due to its struggling turnaround, he is hesitant to sell. Cramer praised Amazon for its strong performance under Andy Jassy and reiterated his long-standing bullish stance on Nvidia, emphasizing its importance in the AI sector and advising investors to own, not trade, the stock.

articles.title: Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Are Souring on Nike—But Apple’s CEO Is Doubling Down
articles.summary: Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan are among major banks lowering their ratings on Nike due to concerns about the brand's long-term performance and declining stock value, which has reached a ten-year low. Despite this, Apple CEO Tim Cook, a Nike board member, recently purchased 25,000 shares, signaling confidence. The company is working to revitalize struggling brands like Converse amidst a tougher competitive landscape.
